compiled_state.title: release.sh: marketplace.json dso ref not updated atomically with tag -> desync window between tag and ref
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
After running release.sh for v1.13.0, marketplace.json still had dso source.ref='main' because updating the ref requires a separate manual 'cutover commit' (story 3bca-5f4e). This creates a window — potentially lasting days — where the v1.13.0 tag exists on origin but consumers who run /plugin marketplace update still resolve to main HEAD, not the tag.

## Expected Behavior
After release.sh completes (tag created + pushed), the dso entry in marketplace.json should be atomically pinned to the new tag in the same commit or immediately after, with no manual step required.

## Actual Behavior
release.sh creates the tag and bumps plugin.json version, but does not update marketplace.json's dso source.ref. A separate cutover commit is required, which can be forgotten or delayed.

## Risk
If a maintainer forgets the cutover commit after a release:
- Tag exists on origin (e.g., v1.14.0)
- marketplace.json dso.source.ref still = 'v1.13.0' (or 'main')
- Consumers on the stable channel get stale content indefinitely
- No automated alert exists for this desync

## Fix Direction
Add a step to release.sh that:
1. After creating and pushing the tag, patches marketplace.json dso.source.ref to the new tag value
2. Validates the JSON (python3 -m json.tool)
3. Commits and pushes the marketplace.json change in the same release run
This makes the tag + ref update atomic from the maintainer's perspective (single script run).

comments.body: Investigation (INTERMEDIATE, opus): tag-release.sh bumps version fields in plugin.json and marketplace.json but does NOT update the dso channel ref to point at the new tag. Result: after release, tag v<X.Y.Z> exists on origin but marketplace.json dso channel ref still points at previous release — persistent desync, not just a window. Fix: update tag-release.sh to also rewrite .claude-plugin/marketplace.json dso channel ref to v<VERSION> as part of the bump commit; optionally switch 'git push --follow-tags' to 'git push --atomic origin main refs/tags/...' to close network-level window. ESCALATE: release process change requires user approval; exact fix location depends on tag-release.sh internals (not read in investigation).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: USER_APPROVAL: User approved fix for this ticket (2026-04-19).
comments.author: Test
